74018.subject: Your AI model got 4 new senses. What comes next?
74018.body:
The Atlas

–Р–≤—В–Њ—А: GenAI Works


–Р–≤—В–Њ—А: GenAI Works

    
    
    –Я—А–Њ—З–Є—В–∞–є—В–µ —Ж—О —Б—В–∞—В—В—О –љ–∞ LinkedIn, —Й–Њ–± –њ—А–Є—Ф–і–љ–∞—В–Є—Б—П –і–Њ –Њ–±–≥–Њ–≤–Њ—А–µ–љ–љ—П
–Я—А–Њ—З–Є—В–∞—В–Є –љ–∞ LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/your-ai-model-got-4-new-senses-what-comes-next-genai-works-wdlxf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3B0cOnpcgVTvOuv592lm4FeA%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=2LqzlI_DjIPIg1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mpygb7r4~w6-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mpygb7r4-w6
  
Welcome back! This week starts with a free tool that shows exactly how much of ClaudeвАЩs context window has already been used. Then comes an open-source AI security scanner that caught 98% of vulnerabilities traditional tools missed. Next is a new embedding model that can search across text, images, audio, and video from a single query. NVIDIA introduces a new approach to spatial reasoning, helping agents know exactly where to click, locate, or grab. In today's Atlas Newsletter: How to see your Claude token usage in real time? A free Chrome extension called Claude Counter shows your context window consumption, remaining capacity and session limits directly inside Claude, so you stop hitting the wall mid-task. Why did Arm's AI security tool find 16x more vulnerabilities than traditional scanners? Metis uses AI with retrieval-augmented generation to reason about how code actually behaves across files and logic flows, catching 98% of known firmware vulnerabilities versus 6% for standard static analysis tools. Google built one embedding model that understands everything: Gemini Embedding 2 puts text, images, audio and video into a single vector space, so a spoken question can retrieve a document and a screenshot can retrieve a video without separate pipelines. Why locating objects in images is harder than it sounds?: LocateAnything uses parallel box decoding to predict object coordinates in one step rather than sequentially, hitting 12.7 boxes per second on a single H100 versus 1.1 for comparable models. PS: Want to share your AI tool or company news with our 14M+ community? Get in touch. How can you monitor your Claude usage in real time? How many Claude tokens have you used in your current session? Most users have no idea. They only find out when Claude suddenly reaches the limit during a long research task, coding session, or multi-document analysis. Stop guessing. Start knowing. A free open-source Chrome extension called Claude Counter helps solve this. A must-have for every Claude user. It shows your token consumption, remaining usage limits, and context window capacity directly inside Claude. Setup starts like this: Visit the GitHub repository and download the ZIP file. Open Chrome and go to Extensions вЖТ Manage Extensions. Enable Developer Mode in the top-right corner. Want the complete step-by-step setup guide? Write TOKEN in the comments and

74017.subject: Anthropic Expands Restricted Cybersecurity Program
74017.body:
Daily Pre-IPO Briefing

StartEngine Private (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VW85Wr8YpkprW8fjNTV1yv3bHW7jDgJ35PSPvwM7WCR63lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3lcW6szBw32bcP_4W7VGltx7NbQKPW6wgp2N3f7YVYW3Ms5z44cjVfDW7j060f4yVHJ6W2kjfzF5XbMb_W7WSs356-6bq6W6lJkcd4bqk_MW6dGdGJ5TP18fW9k-HjR5lFZpnV-hSwq6J15ggW3pkZzg6D0B51N2FDxZ2bjXTYW3tyLk05G1pZlW8Y_TRV2TD47DW226xdw2Z9GmgN59LFqjG3tHTN7hVVqkpXksBW34wZlV80KmsSN5rCdD-BL0vgN1DwN2w3snD8W108gwk543_mTd9RcrP04 )

Daily Pre-IPO Briefing (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VW85Wr8YpkprW8fjNTV1yv3bHW7jDgJ35PSPvwM7WCR63lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3lcW6szBw32bcP_4W7VGltx7NbQKPW6wgp2N3f7YVYW3Ms5z44cjVfDW7j060f4yVHJ6W2kjfzF5XbMb_W7WSs356-6bq6W6lJkcd4bqk_MW6dGdGJ5TP18fW9k-HjR5lFZpnV-hSwq6J15ggW3pkZzg6D0B51N2FDxZ2bjXTYW3tyLk05G1pZlW8Y_TRV2TD47DW226xdw2Z9GmgN59LFqjG3tHTN7hVVqkpXksBW34wZlV80KmsSN5rCdD-BL0vgN1DwN2w3snD8W108gwk543_mTd9RcrP04 )

Hi Oleg,

We all know the top story in the private markets right now: Anthropic, OpenAI, and SpaceX (all StartEngine Private (https://dxszHp04.na2.hubspotlinks.com/Ctc/DR+113/dxszHp04/VW85Wr8YpkprW8fjNTV1yv3bHW7jDgJ35PSPvwM7WCR63lcq-W6N1vHY6lZ3lcW6szBw32bcP_4W7VGltx7NbQKPW6wgp2N3f7YVYW3Ms5z44cjVfDW7j060f4yVHJ6W2kjfzF5XbMb_W7WSs356-6bq6W6lJkcd4bqk_MW6dGdGJ5TP18fW9k-HjR5lFZpnV-hSwq6J15ggW3pkZzg6D0B51N2FDxZ2bjXTYW3tyLk05G1pZlW8Y_TRV2TD47DW226xdw2Z9GmgN59LFqjG3tHTN7hVVqkpXksBW34wZlV80KmsSN5rCdD-BL0vgN1DwN2w3snD8W108gwk543_mTd9RcrP04 ) alums) are planning potentially market-moving IPOs.¬є

But in todayвАЩs Daily Pre-IPO Briefing, we wanted to highlight some other notable headlines beyond these pending public debuts:

- Anthropic expanded access to Project Glasswing by adding an additional 150 partners in over 15 countries. About 200 organizations worldwide are now testing Mythos, Anthropic's cybersecurity AI model for identifying software vulnerabilities in codebases.¬≤
- Kraken is preparing to launch the first CFTC-regulated perpetual futures contracts in the U.S. within the next 30 days. Hailed as the вАЬlifeblood of crypto liquidity,вАЭ global crypto perpetual swaps generated $60T in trading volume In 2025 alone.¬≥
- Lambda Labs closed an oversubscribed $1B senior credit facility, with JPMorgan serving as the lead arranger. Lambda will use the funding to deploy the latest generation of NVIDIA's AI hardware and expand its data center capacity.вБі

A confidential filing does not guarantee that an IPO will occur. This report does not imply specific results for investors with exposure to these companies via StartEngine Private. Past performance is not indicative of future results. Additionally, there's no guarantee that revenue, funding, or valuation will continue to grow for these companies, or that they will be profitable.

IPOs may capture the spotlight. But behind the scenes, private companies continue launching products, expanding infrastructure, reaching new milestones, and opening new markets.

We'll keep tracking the daily headlines that could shape tomorrow's

74015.subject: рЯФЈ The Daily: Blockchain Association, former security officials urge Senate to pass Clarity Act
74015.body:
Cardano's Charles Hoskinson warns of 'wave of failures' after TapTools wind-down | Mastercard expands stablecoin settlement options with USDC, PYUSD and RLUSD | Kraken to offer tokenized US IPO access for global retail investors via xStocks | Binance winds down centralized NFT service, gives users one month to withdraw assets |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Kyle Baird

Presented by

LMAX ( https://www.theblock.co/lmax?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Happy Wednesday! *Bitcoin* slipped below $66,000 this morning as analysts pointed ( https://www.theblock.co/post/403454/bitcoin-dips-below-66000?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) to heavy ETF outflows, Middle East tensions, and lingering fallout from *Strategy's* *BTC* sale.

In today's newsletter, former national security and law enforcement officials urge the *Senate* to pass the *Clarity Act* , *Cardano's Charles Hoskinson* warns of a "wave of failures," *Mastercard* expands its stablecoin settlement options, and more.

Meanwhile, *Sen. Lummis* said ( https://www.theblock.co/post/403539/sen-lummis-jpmorgan-ceo-jamie-dimon-hasnt-read-clarity-act-remarks-distasteful?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) *JPMorgan* CEO *Jamie Dimon* hasn't read the *Clarity Act* , calling his recent remarks about *Coinbase* CEO *Brian Armstrong* "distasteful."

Plus, *Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent* backed ( https://www.theblock.co/post/403563/bessent-backs-summer-push-clarity-act-bitcoin-reserve-moving-deliberate-speed?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) a summer push for passage of the *Clarity Act* , adding that the department is moving at a "deliberate speed" to establish a strategic *bitcoin* reserve.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read вАФ and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*рЯПЫпЄП Blockchain Association urges Senate to pass Clarity Act with letter from 160 former security officials*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/403450/blockchain-association-letter-senate?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

The *Blockchain Association* urged the *Senate* to pass the *Clarity Act* , backing its push with a letter signed by 160 former national security and law enforcement officials.

* The former officials argued the bill would strengthen law enforcement capabilities and improve ef

74013.subject: Ethereum Breaks. Saylor Blinks.
74013.body:
----------
_**Your Weekly Deep Dive into The World of Crypto**_

**June 3, 2026 ¬Ј ****5 Min Read**

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/dddf2f20-4042-4b28-b237-fd6d7f5b23aa/image.png?t=1780466800)
Caption: Market Snapshot¬† ¬Ј As of June 3_¬† |¬† _BTC Price _¬†|¬† _YTD Return _¬†|¬† _Fear & Greed _¬†|¬† _ETF Flows (Weekly)¬†_¬†_

## рЯУ°** THIS WEEK IN CRYPTO**

**GM, Readers!**

This week, crypto and the rest of the market filed for divorce. The S&P 500 hit its 19th all-time high of 2026, the Dow crossed 51,000, and Bitcoin slid to $67,000.

Spot Bitcoin ETFs hit a record 10-day outflow streak. Ether ETFs ran 14 and counting. Ether broke $2,000. And a U.S. court order forced Circle to freeze $12.6M of stablecoins inside an Ethereum privacy protocol.

_The AI trade is having a great year. Crypto is having a week._

_**Let's get into itвА¶**_


----------View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/8132e416-70db-495d-8fec-7c68d66b46d5/image.png?t=1778654852)
Caption: 

----------
# рЯРЛ THE BIG WHALE MOVES

### **1. ****Ethereum's Worst Week of 2026**

Ether broke below $2,000 on May 28 for the first time since late March, capping a brutal stretch that has now wiped 32% off ETH's year-to-date gains. Spot Ether ETFs extended their losing streak past 14 sessions, draining hundreds of millions, while the ETH/BTC ratio kept sliding.

The Ethereum Foundation also lost two more high-profile researchers, Carl Beekhuizen and Julian Ma, deepening the talent-flight narrative. The one bright spot: BitMine, Tom Lee's corporate buyer, kept hoovering up ETH, lifting its stash above $11 billion. Conviction is still there, just not in the price.

_**Meanwhile, Bitcoin saw its own corporate headline:**_ Strategy (formerly MicroStrategy) sold 32 BTC (around $2.5M) for the first time since 2022, as mentioned in a[ June 1 filing](https://assets.contentstack.io/v3/assets/bltf8d808d9b8cebd37/blt01aedf36c9f1b5b3/6a1cdb95487e7818fe49dd85/form-8-k_06-01-2026.pdf), to fund preferred stock dividends, a symbolic break from the "never sell" playbook.

View image: (https://media.beehiiv.com/cdn-cgi/image/fit=scale-down,format=auto,onerror=redirect,quality=80/uploads/asset/file/b6312343-3c6f-4fec-bf96-c18493c14410/image.png?t=1780466894)
Caption: _Chart: Bitcoin price over the past 7 days_

### **2. ****Circle Drops the Freeze Button on $12.6M**

On May 30, USDC issuer Circle blacklisted the smart contract behind Zama's confidential USDC (cUSDC) wrapper, freezing roughly $12.6 million inside. The trigger was a federal court order tied to a class-action against Overnight Finance, whose founder allegedly moved $15.77M from the treasury and parked $12.4M in Zama's pool on May 11.

_**The catch? **_Zama and most of its users had nothing to do with the alleged fraud. Innocent cUSDC holders w

74012.subject: Want to know the future? DonвАЩt trust the stockmarket
74012.body:
<link rel=stylesheet href=https://marber-cdn.economist.com/foundations/latest/css/font-face.css>
 The Economist

 
 


 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTZ9AAcAAAAAAjzMpLWprNcd9pzFdKugAM__RdmyDiaaE1PRp4rUz6yWqmThJdD2ADU-p_lWWRkSVQLvXvZ-fLJh4Rgz_Jn43C8RMH7wk3ZXndZcxY8e0b_J 

June 3rd 2026

 

 
 
 
The Economist Today

 Our best journalism, handpicked each day


 
 
 

 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTZ9AAcAAAAAAjzMpLWqsYUJGatb-mqBfMGlcsEXcX5hg4ZIs4L-CRTodiZbS87QQTgGg_Ru2Z5Sn-SvkHfnaPn9iVN_FsARTwdU2_a0ejQRyVjEqeTOy8Tg 

https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTZ9AAcAAAAAAjzMpLWr5av7uHLtMHrW-ZUKmALGDFptk9tDA8IS_MmPYrZxlrRO3nQokmEth1gZ0QdXO678nAS1nemCHZUxq5uOqowzzSMcwtPhjo5RLxKC 



 
 

Lane Greene

Senior digital editor



Many financial economists believe in the efficient-markets hypothesis: that stockmarket investors accurately absorb new information and reprice shares to reflect companies’ underlying profitability. But 
https://click.e.economist.com/?qs=ABB7InYiOjEsImQiOjQ4OTZ9AAcAAAAAAjzMpLWsviTyx78Hy6tw_jVV573ycEInWfKSVy6mdLZAG8JyzvIQMDuGYDFBQe77s1T2soWlY5uFn1ILfQp-6Kh9gayAUZXBTIaYDhE 
Buttonwood takes a critical view , showing why markets can be much more volatile (often on the upside) than efficient-market reasoning can explain.
